Title: TDR INTERACTING PROTEIN 3, encoding a PHD-finger transcription factor, regulates Ubisch bodies and pollen wall formation in rice

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PHD-finger protein; canonical Cys4-His-Cys3 PHD domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in root, stem, leaf, sheath, with relatively higher expression in anther; in anthers strongly expressed in tapetum and microspores from stages 8a to 10, reduced from stage 11 onward
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in tapetum and microspores during anther development; high at dyad, tetrad and microspore stages
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for subcellular localization; native TIP3 promoter (pTIP3, 2225 bp upstream) for GUS; native genomic fragment for complementation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pollen exine and Ubisch bodies in anther locules
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms the protective outer pollen wall required for viable pollen formation and male fertility
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and transport-associated regulation of sporopollenin precursor production/deposition; tapetum programmed cell death
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal Ubisch body morphogenesis, pollen wall/exine formation and male fertility; loss causes delayed tapetum degeneration, abnormal Ubisch bodies, no pollen wall formation and male sterility
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator required for Ubisch body formation and pollen exine deposition in rice anthers
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; likely indirect/complex-mediated regulation through interaction with TDR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Disruption of TIP3 altered expression of genes for tapetum development/degradation and sporopollenin precursor biosynthesis/transport; positively associated with normal expression of MSP1, GAMYB, UDT1/TDR/EAT1 module, CP1, AP25, AP37, DPW, CYP703A3, CYP704B2, OsC6, OsABCG15 and OsABCG26
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental cues during anther/tapetum and microspore development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with TDR in the nucleus in vivo; Y2H also detected interaction of TIP3 PHD domain with EAT1 and TIP2 in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants; anther developmental stage is critical for phenotype interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; TEM/SEM cytology; TUNEL; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; GUS reporter; yeast transactivation; Y2H; BiFC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TIP3 was identified by map-based cloning as LOC_Os03g50780, and a frameshift mutation caused complete male sterility in tip3. 2. Complementation restored fertility, while CRISPR knockout recapitulated the male-sterile phenotype, confirming TIP3 identity and function. 3. TIP3 encodes a nuclear PHD-finger transcription factor with transcriptional activation activity. 4. TIP3 is highly expressed in tapetum and microspores during key stages of exine formation. 5. tip3 mutants show delayed tapetal programmed cell death, abnormal Ubisch bodies, absent pollen exine and no mature pollen. 6. TIP3 physically interacts with TDR in vivo and affects expression of genes involved in tapetum degeneration and sporopollenin precursor biosynthesis/transport.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes complete male sterility, though vegetative growth appears normal
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful tool for manipulating male fertility in hybrid rice breeding; likely applicable for male-sterility line development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not demonstrate direct DNA binding targets of TIP3. Although Y2H showed interactions with TDR, EAT1 and TIP2, BiFC confirmed only TIP3-TDR in vivo. TIP3 is distinct from other known PHD-finger regulators such as PTC1/MS1 clade and may function in a unique monocot-specific manner.
